description: Perfective 'ki' presents an event as a completed whole at its tense's reference time. The counterpart 'si' (IPFV) shows the event from within while it is still in progress; an unmarked verb commits to neither view.
articulatory_notes: The back of the tongue closes against the soft palate for 'k', then opens onto high-front unrounded 'i'. The particle has one syllable, under full stress.
sound_symbolism: The complete velar closure and quick release give 'ki' a clipped contour. That small boundary can help a learner remember the complete-event reading.
usage_notes: Aspect occupies the rank between tense and voice. Bare 'ki' means have done, 'to ki' means had done, and 'so ki' means will have done. No clause carries two aspect particles.
